lesson.menu_desc: Starting and keeping a conversation going -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 -- Small Talk & Networking at Events -- Keeping a Conversation Going
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 4 -- Small Talk & Networking at Events

listenings.text: Good afternoon, everyone, and welcome to the networking break. This is the moment to meet new people, so please do not be shy. Introduce yourself and break the ice with a simple question. If you are not sure what to say, here are some safe topics: ask about the talks, ask about travel, or ask about the city. These are easy topics that everyone can share. My best tip is this: ask open questions instead of yes or no questions, and show real interest in the other person. When you find something you have in common, the conversation becomes natural and easy. So go ahead, walk around, and enjoy the conversations. You never know which contact today will become an important partner tomorrow. Thank you, and have a great break.
